# RHEL kernel boot order

Inspect the current GRUB2 default with `grubby --default-kernel`. List
available kernels with `grubby --info=ALL`. Set a new default with
`grubby --set-default=/boot/vmlinuz-X.Y.Z-...`.

Never switch kernels on WiFi-only systems without first verifying that
the network driver is available in the target kernel — losing WiFi on
boot turns the host into a paperweight.
